license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.x88frzw
title: Neural progenitor expansion
author: Celeste M M. Karch

steps_raw.instruction: Using the pre-coated PLO-laminin plate, aspirate laminin from 2-3 wells. Remove supernatant from 15 mL conical tube containing 2 wells of neural rosette clusters. See protocol below. Neural Rosette Formation and Selection by Celeste M M. Karch
Add appropriate amount of neural induction media (NIM) to achieve a final volume of 2 mL per well. Pipet clusters up and down breaking them into 1/4 or 1/5 the size of the rosette cluster.
Add 2 mL of neural rosette cluster suspension to each well and incubate at 37 °C , 5% CO 2 and 95% humidified chamber for 24:00:00 .
Examine adherent cells under microscope, aspirate off medium and replace with 2 mL of fresh neural induction medium daily for 3-5 days or until they reach ~80% confluent.
Make 12 mL of DMEM/F12 supplemented with 10% FBS by adding 2 mL of FBS to 10 mL of DMEM/F12 to inhibit trypsin activity (termed “Complete Media”).
Remove medium and rinse cells with 1 mL/well of DMEM/F12 .
Harvest cells by adding 1 mL/well of 0.05% trypsin and incubate at 37 °C for 00:03:00 .
Inhibit trypsin activity by adding 4 mL of DMEM/F12 supplemented with 10% FBS to each well.

Collect cells in 15mL conical tube. Centrifuge at 750 rpm for 00:03:00 . To minimize bubbles and increase cell yield, add 1mL Complete Media to 15mL conical tube. After trypsinization, add 3mL DMEM/F12 to collect cells and transfer to 15mL conical tube.
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend in 12 mL of NIM, pipet at least 3 times to break up large clumps. Small clumps and single cells are acceptable to passage.
Passage cells to pre-coated PLO-lamin plates (see above) by adding 2 mLs/well of suspended cells for a total of 6 wells.
Feed with 2 mL of fresh NIM daily until they reach 85-95% confluency.

IMPORTANT: NPCs are best maintained by passaging at a 1:3 dilution. Passaging cells that are more dilute will result in slow growth. Cells will typically become confluent after 2-4 days when plated as a 1:3 dilution. If NPCs are observed to slow in their growth, passage cells and replate without dilution.
